title: quercus sessilis var. macroloba
intro: Quercus sessilis var. macroloba, also known as Mexican white oak or Escobet oak, is a variety of evergreen tree native to Mexico, characterized by its broad leaves and white bark. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high levels of flavonoids and tannins. Consuming parts of the plant may provide several health benefits, such as lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and boosting the immune system. Mexican herbal preparations often utilize the plant, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to support cardiovascular health, digestive function, and respiratory well-being.
